_description: Canonical agent registry. The 'description' field mirrors the full frontmatter description in templates/agents/{name}.md and is the source of truth. Short 'blurb' is what appears in README/CLAUDE.md tables. Update both places together, then run tests/check-agent-registry.sh.

categories.analysis: Read-only review agents — produce structured reports, do not modify code
categories.planning: Read-only planning specialists — analyze a story and produce structured plan inputs (questions, work breakdown, risks, test strategy)
categories.implementation: Can modify files (build phases, tests, documentation, refactor pass)
categories.meta: Operates on the framework itself OR orchestrates other agents (coordinators)

agents.name: architect
agents.blurb: System design review, architecture patterns, scalability
agents.description: Reviews system design decisions, validates architecture patterns, and identifies structural risks before implementation

agents.name: code-reviewer
agents.blurb: Review diffs for bugs, security, performance, design principles, code smells, conventions
agents.description: Reviews code changes for bugs, security issues, performance problems, design principles (pure functions, SRP), code smells, and convention violations before merge

agents.name: code-smell-reviewer
agents.blurb: Code smells: long methods, magic numbers, primitive obsession, dead code (cites `code-smells` rule)
agents.description: Reviews changed code for classic code smells — long methods, magic numbers, primitive obsession, data clumps, feature envy, dead code, comments-as-deodorant, speculative generality. Cites the `code-smells` rule

agents.name: dry-reviewer
agents.blurb: Duplication and extraction opportunities (cites `dry` rule)
agents.description: Reviews changed code for true duplication — repeated knowledge, repeated logic, repeated structural patterns appearing 3+ times. Cites the `dry` rule. Distinct from refactor-advisor (broader) and code-smell-reviewer (other smells)

agents.name: purity-reviewer
agents.blurb: Pure functions, side effects, query/command separation, SRP (cites `purity` rule)
agents.description: Reviews changed code for impure functions, hidden side effects, query/command separation violations, input mutation, hidden state reads, and SRP violations at function and class level. Cites the `purity` rule

agents.name: complexity-reviewer
agents.blurb: Function length, cyclomatic complexity, nesting, parameters (cites `complexity` rule)
agents.description: Reviews changed code for excessive function length, cyclomatic complexity, nesting depth, parameter count, and branch density. Cites the `complexity` rule

agents.name: frontend-architecture-reviewer
agents.blurb: Component composition, state management, hook discipline, data flow, render-perf architecture (cites `frontend-architecture` rule)
agents.description: Reviews changed frontend code for component composition, state management, hook discipline, data flow, render performance, module boundaries, and SSR/hydration concerns. Cites the `frontend-architecture` rule. Distinct from ui-ux-reviewer (visual/a11y/design system)

agents.name: architecture-reviewer
agents.blurb: Layer dependency direction, cross-module reach, circular deps, god modules, public-API leaks (cites `architecture-layering` rule)
agents.description: Reviews changed code for layer-dependency violations, cross-module reach, circular dependencies, god modules, public-API leaks, and structural patterns. Cites the `architecture-layering` rule. Distinct from `architect` (which plans) — this agent reviews code against architectural rules

agents.name: api-layering-reviewer
agents.blurb: Controller/service/repository separation, validation placement, error contract, idempotency (cites `api-layering` rule)
agents.description: Reviews API code for controller/service/repository separation, validation layer placement, error contract consistency, idempotency, and versioning discipline. Cites the `api-layering` rule. Distinct from `api-designer` (designs API surface) and `api-routes` rule (per-route concerns)

agents.name: security-auditor
agents.blurb: OWASP audit, credentials, dependencies, compliance
agents.description: Audits code for security vulnerabilities, credential exposure, PII leakage, auth bypass, CSRF, CORS, SSRF, dependency risks, and compliance issues

agents.name: refactor-advisor
agents.blurb: Duplication, complexity, extraction opportunities
agents.description: Analyzes code for duplication, complexity, and architectural improvements — reports only, does not modify code

agents.name: devops-engineer
agents.blurb: CI/CD, containers, infrastructure, monitoring
agents.description: Reviews CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure configuration, deployment strategies, and operational readiness

agents.name: ui-ux-reviewer
agents.blurb: Accessibility, design consistency, responsiveness
agents.description: Reviews UI components for accessibility, design system compliance, visual consistency, responsive behavior, and user experience patterns

agents.name: performance-optimizer
agents.blurb: Bundle size, queries, rendering, caching, memory
agents.description: Profiles application performance — bundle size, query efficiency, render optimization, memory usage, and caching opportunities

agents.name: api-designer
agents.blurb: Endpoint design, schemas, versioning, DX
agents.description: Reviews API design for consistency, RESTful conventions, schema validation, versioning, and developer experience

agents.name: database-architect
agents.blurb: Schema, normalization, indexes, migration safety
agents.description: Reviews database schema design, migration strategy, query patterns, indexing, and data integrity constraints

agents.name: test-writer
agents.blurb: Generate tests following project conventions and factories
agents.description: Generates test cases for changed code following project test conventions and data factories

agents.name: documentation-writer
agents.blurb: API docs, READMEs, architecture docs, setup guides
agents.description: Generates and updates project documentation — API docs, architecture overviews, setup guides, and inline documentation

agents.name: requirements-clarifier
agents.blurb: Hunts ambiguity in story before planning — surfaces open questions, undefined terms, missing AC, conflicting requirements
agents.description: Hunts ambiguity in a story or task description before planning starts. Surfaces open questions, undefined terms, missing acceptance criteria, conflicting requirements, and assumed-but-unstated constraints

agents.name: scope-decomposer
agents.blurb: Breaks a story into atomic, sequenced steps with parallelism groups and dependencies
agents.description: Breaks a story or task into atomic, independently-testable steps with explicit sequencing and dependencies. Identifies what can run in parallel and what must happen in order

agents.name: risk-assessor
agents.blurb: Identifies rollback paths, blast radius, breaking-change and migration risk; proposes mitigations
agents.description: Identifies rollback paths, blast radius, breaking-change risk, data-migration danger, and operational risk for a planned change. Output feeds into the plan so risky steps get extra scrutiny

agents.name: test-strategy-planner
agents.blurb: Decides what tests at what level (unit / integration / e2e / contract / property) per planned step
agents.description: Decides what tests at what level (unit / integration / e2e / contract / property) for a planned change. Output feeds into the plan so the build phase generates the right tests

agents.name: scaffold-implementer
agents.blurb: Build phase 1 — file structure, types, signatures, stubs (no logic)
agents.description: Build phase 1 specialist — creates the file structure, types/interfaces, function signatures, and empty stubs for a planned change. Does not implement logic; produces the skeleton the next phases fill in. Constrained by all relevant `.claude/rules/`

agents.name: happy-path-implementer
agents.blurb: Build phase 2 — core successful flow logic (defers errors and edges)
agents.description: Build phase 2 specialist — fills in the core successful flow for stubs created by scaffold-implementer. Implements the "everything works" version. Defers error handling, edge cases, and validation to the edge-case-implementer phase. Constrained by all relevant `.claude/rules/`

agents.name: edge-case-implementer
agents.blurb: Build phase 3 — input validation, error handling, edge data, defensive code
agents.description: Build phase 3 specialist — adds input validation, error handling, edge-case handling, and defensive code to the happy-path implementation. Tightly bound to the `error-handling`, `auth-security`, and `data-protection` rules. Constrained by all relevant `.claude/rules/`

agents.name: refactor-pass-implementer
agents.blurb: Build phase 6 (final) — actively applies code-smells/dry/purity/complexity rules; preempts review findings
agents.description: Build phase 5 specialist (final pass before review) — actively applies code-quality rules to the just-implemented code: simplifies, extracts, dedupes, reduces complexity. Tightly bound to `code-smells`, `dry`, `purity`, `complexity` rules. Goal is that the review-coordinator finds little to flag

agents.name: framework-improver
agents.blurb: Self-improvement — update CLAUDE.md, rules, settings, agents
agents.description: Self-improvement agent — analyzes project patterns and updates CLAUDE.md, rules, settings, and agent configurations to improve AI effectiveness

agents.name: review-coordinator
agents.blurb: Synthesizes parallel reviewer output — dedupes, filters, persists state across iterations
agents.description: Synthesizes findings from parallel reviewer agents into one consolidated report — dedupes, filters false positives, applies cross-iteration state, classifies the diff into a risk tier, and decides which sub-agents to spawn

agents.name: planner-coordinator
agents.blurb: Orchestrates planning specialists — classifies scope, spawns parallel waves, synthesizes one plan
agents.description: Orchestrates planning specialists (requirements-clarifier, scope-decomposer, risk-assessor, test-strategy-planner, plus architect/api-designer/database-architect when relevant) and synthesizes their outputs into a single implementation plan

agents.name: build-coordinator
agents.blurb: Orchestrates build phases sequentially — scaffold → happy-path → edge-case → tests → docs → refactor
agents.description: Orchestrates build phases sequentially — scaffold → happy-path → edge-case → tests → docs → refactor — using the plan from planner-coordinator. Each phase is one specialist; the coordinator manages sequencing, state, and resumption
